title: Tools chosen for reliability under load.
description: Production usage, constraints, and where each skill earns its place — ordered by what the market is hiring for right now.

items.name: TypeScript
items.overview: Typed contracts across APIs, workers, AI pipelines, and frontend surfaces.
items.productionUsage: End-to-end typing for payment logic, LLM I/O, and safer refactors in production systems.
items.challenges: Balancing strictness with delivery speed across two production codebases.
items.favoriteUse: Domain models that make invalid payment and AI response states hard to represent.

items.name: Node.js
items.overview: Primary runtime for production APIs, workers, and real-time services.
items.productionUsage: Core backend runtime for the financing engine, webhooks, and AI job workers.
items.challenges: Keeping long-running services resilient under payment and queue load.
items.favoriteUse: Async service boundaries with clear failure modes.

items.name: OpenAI
items.overview: Production LLM integrations and prompt engineering — content generation, sentiment analysis, and decision support, not demos.
items.productionUsage: AI content pipelines, review sentiment analysis, and send-time decisioning that lifted engagement 40% and saved 15+ hours weekly.
items.challenges: Latency, cost control, and output quality at real campaign scale — not just a single prompt.
items.favoriteUse: Turning prompt engineering into repeatable, queued, cost-aware product workflows.

items.name: PostgreSQL
items.overview: Primary relational store for the payment financing engine — schema, indexing, and query performance under load.
items.productionUsage: Transactional source of truth for financing, installment, and ledger data.
items.challenges: Indexing and query tuning that cut API P99 latency 60% and DB CPU utilization 35%.
items.favoriteUse: Query plans that stay predictable as payment volume grows.

items.name: React
items.overview: Component-driven dashboards for campaign analytics and lead management.
items.productionUsage: Frontend surfaces for AI campaign performance and reputation monitoring.
items.challenges: Keeping dashboards fast with real-time data and frequent re-renders.
items.favoriteUse: Composable views over async, frequently-changing product data.

items.name: Angular
items.overview: Frontend delivery for complex product workflows and admin surfaces.
items.productionUsage: Application shell, forms, and operational UI for both production products.
items.challenges: Keeping large frontend surfaces fast and maintainable over years.
items.favoriteUse: Structured UI for money and ops workflows that cannot afford ambiguity.

items.name: AWS
items.overview: Cloud infrastructure for compute, storage, and delivery of production services.
items.productionUsage: EC2, S3, and Lambda for hosting, media storage, and event-driven jobs.
items.challenges: Keeping environments boring, observable, and cost-aware.
items.favoriteUse: Reliable deploy and runtime paths teams can trust.

items.name: Docker
items.overview: Containerized services and CI/CD pipelines for consistent, repeatable deploys.
items.productionUsage: Docker + Jenkins CI/CD cutting release time 65% with zero-downtime deployments.
items.challenges: Image size, build caching, and parity between local and production environments.
items.favoriteUse: Making "works on my machine" a non-issue.

items.name: Redis
items.overview: Caching, coordination, and queue infrastructure for high-throughput paths.
items.productionUsage: Latency-sensitive caching and background job coordination behind BullMQ.
items.challenges: Cache invalidation and backpressure under burst traffic.
items.favoriteUse: Cutting hot-path latency without hiding consistency issues.

items.name: Kafka
items.overview: Event-driven messaging alongside Redis/BullMQ for durable, ordered event delivery.
items.productionUsage: Event orchestration for payment and reconciliation events that need ordered, replayable delivery.
items.challenges: Consumer lag and partitioning decisions under bursty payment traffic.
items.favoriteUse: Decoupling producers from consumers without losing event order.

items.name: BullMQ
items.overview: Reliable background processing for payments, AI jobs, and async workflows.
items.productionUsage: Worker pipelines processing hundreds of thousands of daily jobs at 99.9% event delivery reliability.
items.challenges: Retries, idempotency, and dead-letter recovery across job types.
items.favoriteUse: Turning brittle request/response flows into durable work.

items.name: Kubernetes
items.overview: Container orchestration for scaling and managing production service deployments.
items.productionUsage: Deployment and scaling policies for containerized backend services.
items.favoriteUse: Predictable rollouts and self-healing service instances.

items.name: Stripe
items.overview: Stripe Connect OBO flows, Terminal S700, and fee logic — light exposure during Practina, now the core payment rail at Credee.
items.productionUsage: Payout orchestration and reconciliation across 1,000+ merchants, plus in-person payments via Terminal S700.
items.challenges: Idempotent webhook handling and fee edge cases at merchant scale.
items.favoriteUse: Designing payment systems that survive reality.

items.name: MySQL
items.overview: Transactional data store for product state, payments, and audit trails.
items.productionUsage: Source of truth for financial and workflow data alongside PostgreSQL.
items.challenges: Schema evolution and query performance under growing load.
items.favoriteUse: Clear transactional boundaries around money and status changes.

items.name: Express
items.overview: HTTP layer for REST APIs, webhooks, and service ingress.
items.productionUsage: API gateway surface for payment, AI, and integration traffic.
items.challenges: Consistent validation, auth, and error shape across many routes.
items.favoriteUse: Thin controllers over clear domain services.

items.name: Socket.IO
items.overview: Real-time channels for live product updates and collaboration signals.
items.productionUsage: Real-time tracking and notifications that reduced support escalations.
items.challenges: Reconnect behavior and fan-out under concurrent clients.
items.favoriteUse: Making long-running work feel immediate in the UI.
